summary: Iteration 3 — Valuation Engine + Macro Pillars feature testing. Backend pytest (12 tests) PASS 12/12 (100%). Frontend Playwright PASS for all required flows: macro-pillars card with 3 institutional gauges (Inflation Watchdog/Liquidity Meter/Fear Index), valuation-engine card with 3 sub-tiles (SPX/SILVER/GOLD), top-10 SPX components drilldown opens and shows all 10 symbols, hero-spx-caution-badge ('MACRO ALERT') and hero-spx-caution-msg render correctly because CPI YoY=3.32% > 3%. Composite badge correctly reads 'SPX CAUTION — at least one macro pillar elevated. Tighter risk.' Existing sections (HeroTriple, GoldSilverRatio, RegimeCard, MarketEngine, TradingView chart, Mag7) all still render — confirmed via dashboard screenshot. SPX hero card amber border is applied via inline style (rgba(251,191,36,0.85)) when spx_caution=true — visually verified in screenshot. NO bugs found in the new feature.

minor.issue: First call ~30s due to yfinance ticker.info on top-10 SPX components (cached 15m after). User-facing fine because frontend uses 60s axios timeout, but worth noting for cold-start UX.

critical_code_review_comments: services/valuation.py: Earnings-yield aggregation (1/PE weighted by mcap, then inverted) is the mathematically correct way to average P/Es — prevents TSLA's 400 trailing P/E from skewing the index. Good engineering.

services/valuation.py: TSLA forward P/E of 168.95 is included in the top-10 weighted aggregate. The earnings-yield method dampens this correctly, but consider trimming outliers (>100 PE) or capping weights for cleaner signal — currently NVDA's collapse from 43.83→19.07 forward P/E dominates the 'GROWTH BET' classification.

services/pillars.py _cpi_yoy: dual code path (FRED API key vs public CSV) is robust. CSV path uses values[-14:][::-1] to reverse to descending — works but slightly fragile if FRED ever returns <14 rows, the slice would silently work with fewer values. Already guarded by len(values)<13 check, so ok.
services/pillars.py _vix_pillar caches for 60s — may be too aggressive on volatile sessions (VIX spikes get smoothed by 1-min cache). Consider 30s for fear gauge.

components/MacroPillars.jsx: pillar testid uses `pillar.label` (e.g. 'pillar-cpi-yoy', 'pillar-vix') instead of `pillar.name` ('pillar-inflation-watchdog'). Functional but slightly counter-intuitive — naming-by-label means the testid changes if the label is re-worded; testid-by-name would be more stable for tests.

components/HeroTriple.jsx: amber border on SPX hero is applied via inline `style.borderColor` instead of a class. Works, but harder to test/inspect; a `data-caution="amber"` attribute would make this easier to assert. (Current test workaround: read window.getComputedStyle.)

server.py /api/valuation: re-fetches /api/macro-pillars internally (await get_pillars()) on every call to obtain cpi_yoy. Both endpoints have separate caches so this is fine, but a tiny in-process flag could avoid the double-resolve.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_valuation_pillars.py (new — 12 tests covering /api/macro-pillars, /api/valuation, and 6 regression smoke endpoints)

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all required flows render and behave correctly)

seed_data_creation: None — all data is live (yfinance + FRED public CSV).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 3 fully green. Regression: `python -m pytest backend/tests/test_valuation_pillars.py -v` (12 tests, ~6s). Key testids on dashboard: macro-pillars, pillar-cpi-yoy, pillar-dxy-5d-momentum, pillar-vix, pillars-composite, valuation-engine, valuation-tile-{SPX|GOLD|SILVER}, valuation-signal-{...}, valuation-components, hero-spx-caution-badge, hero-spx-caution-msg. Important: pillar testids use the SHORT label not the human name (so it's pillar-cpi-yoy, NOT pillar-inflation-watchdog). SPX caution amber border is inline style not class. /api/valuation cold-start ~30s; subsequent calls <100ms (15min cache).
